mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling from an already low level, the market reads a lower discount-rate path and easier liquidity conditions, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. This signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by at least 0.25 pp over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: If triggered cases do not beat the unconditional 20d return rate, or if negative 20d returns occur in more than 55% of trigger instances, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE reflects deteriorating labor demand and weaker household income growth, which increases recession risk and depresses equity valuations over the next 20 trading days. The effect should appear as UNRATE turns up from a low base.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward returns are positive in a majority of unemployment-rise triggers, or if the signal's rolling 3-year hit rate falls below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in RSAFS and HOUST signals stronger current consumption and housing demand, which supports revenues and near-term equity gains over the next 60 trading days. The joint expansion matters more than either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined positive-slope condition on RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform single-series variants, or if the interaction term does not improve 60d directional accuracy over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in INDPRO indicates weakening industrial output and profit-sensitive demand for cyclical sectors, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over roughly one trading week. This is a short-horizon risk-off signal tied to real activity momentum.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if weekly-style trigger windows produce positive 5d returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the effect reverses in most rolling subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: A high or rising FEDFUNDS rate tightens financial conditions, raises the equity discount rate, and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is strongest when inflation is not falling quickly.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above 0.25% and rising over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if rising-rate episodes produce positive 60d returns more often than negative ones, or if controlling for CPIAUCSL removes the sign of the rate effect.

mechanism_narrative: Upward revisions in GDPC1 indicate the economy is expanding faster than earlier estimates, which should improve earnings expectations and support S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal should work only when the revision is positive, not merely when growth is high.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly vintage revision is positive versus the prior release
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 revisions do not correspond to above-baseline 20d returns, or if negative revisions generate equal-or-better returns than positive revisions.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration in PAYEMS and UNRATE signals that hiring is slowing while unemployment is rising, a classic recession-warning pattern that should pressure equities over the next 60 trading days. The combined labor-market deterioration is more informative than either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction does not improve 60d directional accuracy versus standalone PAYEMS or UNRATE effects, or if triggered cases show persistent positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO after a prior contraction indicates manufacturing stabilization and inventory restocking, which should support cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is a recovery pattern, not a level effect.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises after at least 2 consecutive monthly declines
failure_condition: Reject if rebound episodes fail to outperform unconditional returns, or if positive 20d returns are not more frequent than negative returns after the trigger.
